projectDescription: Apache Wayang is the first open-source cross-platform data processing system that lets you write a pipeline once and run it on any engine — Java Streams, Apache Spark, Flink, PostgreSQL, and more — with a cost-based optimizer that selects the best platform per operator automatically.
keyFeatures: Cross-platform execution: write once, run on Java Streams, Apache Spark, Flink, PostgreSQL, SQLite, Kafka, TensorFlow, and more
Cost-based optimizer that automatically selects the best execution platform per operator
Fluent Java/Scala API (DataQuanta / PlanBuilder / JavaPlanBuilder) for building pipelines declaratively
Python API (PyWay) for building Wayang plans from Python
SQL API powered by Apache Calcite for query-based pipelines
Plugin/platform adapter architecture for adding new execution engines without modifying core
ML-based cost model (wayang-ml) using ONNX runtime for learned plan estimation
Rich operator library: map, filter, flatMap, reduceByKey, join, coGroup, sort, sample, distinct, loop, doWhile, repeat, pageRank, k-means, and more
Cross-cloud storage sources: Amazon S3, Azure Blob, Google Cloud Storage, Apache Iceberg, Parquet, Kafka
Cardinality and selectivity estimation for accurate optimizer decisions
Spatial data processing support via wayang-spatial plugin
Inequality join support via wayang-iejoin plugin
publicApiSurface: WayangContext (org.apache.wayang.core.api) — entry point; register platforms with .withPlugin()
Configuration (org.apache.wayang.core.api) — key-value configuration for context and jobs
JavaPlanBuilder (org.apache.wayang.api) — fluent Java builder for Wayang plans
PlanBuilder (org.apache.wayang.api) — fluent Scala builder for Wayang plans
DataQuanta[T] (org.apache.wayang.api) — lazy data-flow edge with transformation methods
Java.basicPlugin() / Spark.basicPlugin() / Flink.basicPlugin() — platform plugin entry points
DataQuanta#map, filter, flatMap, reduceByKey, groupByKey, join, coGroup, sort, distinct, count, sample, union, intersect, doWhile, repeat
EstimatableCost interface — implement custom cost models for the optimizer
Plugin interface — implement new platform adapters
wayang-submit CLI — execute Wayang applications from the command line
